title: AstralDeep Release Readiness Evidence
description: Schema for one platform report, a bounded platform-exception request, the same-candidate aggregate release decision, or Windows draft-verification provenance. Local parsing is diagnostic; protected native CI independently authorizes decisions.

immutable_reference.$comment: Policy validation percent-decodes and normalizes bundle/member paths, rejects absolute paths, traversal, empty segments where disallowed, and symlinks, and resolves gh/oci identity only through an attestation-verified trust manifest.

approval_payload_sha256.$comment: SHA-256 of compact sorted-key UTF-8 JSON (ensure_ascii=false, sort_keys=true, separators=(',', ':'), allow_nan=false, no trailing newline; reject duplicate keys/non-finite values) containing only candidate_sha, release_id, release_version, requested_tag, target_commit_sha, trusted_decision_immutable_reference, trusted_decision_sha256, controller_digest, publisher_digest, bridge_workflow_sha256, and requester_login. Values are taken from this parent provenance and protected-publisher request.

protected_publisher.$comment: These fields are cross-checks only. Before any tag/release mutation, policy requires equality with independently installed full controller/publisher commits, bridge blob hash, and token-broker policy; re-queries the same-repository protected deployment/payload; requires an allowlisted reviewer different from the requester; and proves the broker granted the scoped publication token only to the exact pinned publisher workflow SHA. Moved-main/modified-publisher or self-declared approval fails before authority is issued. The legacy bridge has no mutation permission and can receive OIDC only after the protected publisher creates its exact tag and proves byte equality with bridge_workflow_sha256.

trusted_decision_reference.$comment: Policy resolves and re-hashes manifest_artifact by canonical current-repository run/artifact/member identity, independently verifies its attestation against protected builder values, parses trusted_release_decision, and requires exact decision/evidence-set/candidate/release/policy/check identity plus current time before valid_until. Self-declared or expired fields never establish readiness.

unavailability_observation.$comment: The always-running control producer records a bounded API/log snapshot for an attempted target job. Policy resolves and re-hashes this reference through an independently verified producer manifest and proves failure occurred before any product test ran; a candidate-authored reason string is insufficient.

endpoint.$comment: The URI parser policy also requires empty userinfo/query/fragment components; request identity belongs in the host or path and archived endpoints never contain credentials.

checks.$comment: The policy validator rejects duplicate check.id values within one platform report, including duplicates whose remaining fields differ. The same check ID may appear in different platform reports.

evidence_exception_request.$comment: These immutable pre-review bytes contain only requester-known scope and identity. Reviewer, approval, expiry, and protected-ledger append facts exist only in the separately attested trusted_exception_approval created after review and registration. Policy rejects guessed/self-declared approval data, requires exact byte identity with that approval manifest, and treats the request as candidate/release-bound.

evidence_set.$comment: The aggregator, in addition to schema validation, requires exact candidate_sha/release identity agreement, one non-duplicated report per required target, no duplicate check.id within a platform report, byte-identical normalized staging identity across passing non-doc reports, no failed check for pass, only policy-enumerated not_applicable outcomes, only unavailable shipping-client checks covered by current requests whose exact bytes have a trusted approval plus protected-ledger registration receipt, no failed-product/backend/docs/staging/trust/policy or Apple first-login exception, and artifact bytes/digests from independently attestation-verified workflow provenance governed by release-trust.schema.json. It derives canonical bundle/GitHub-run-member/GitHub-release-asset/OCI references, recomputes hashes, rejects mutable/path-traversing references, binds platform artifact and runner identity to attested jobs, and compares reachability only to verified stage-deploy outputs. Feature-059 applicability comes only from the candidate capability map: false requires macos_personal_agent_host=not_applicable; true requires it passed plus a structured v2 registration and agent_host_registered acknowledgement; missing/malformed/refused is blocking. Every prior blocks_next_release debt from the exact protected ledger commit must be resolved by a current passing check.

windows_draft_verification_provenance.$comment: Before authority or publication, policy independently verifies trusted_decision.manifest_artifact against release-trust trusted_release_decision and the required protected check, and re-queries protected publisher workflow SHA, token-broker policy, deployment payload/reviewer state, and bridge blob hash; embedded values are cross-checks only. The protected publisher refuses any existing official tag/release/assets, creates exactly v plus strict release_version at the decision SHA, sets release_name equal to that tag and latest_disposition=make_latest_on_publish for shipped-v0.3.0 updater compatibility, obtains a detached bundle only from the byte-pinned legacy bridge, and uploads exactly AstralDeep.exe, SHA256SUMS, and cosign.bundle create-only to a new draft. It re-queries draft_state=true, prerelease=false, asset_count=3, three distinct IDs and constant names; resolves/re-downloads canonical same-repository assets; requires release_name=tag=v plus release_version, the draft EXE digest to equal matrix-tested bytes, and the eventual public transition to make this release the /releases/latest result; parses SHA256SUMS; verifies the bundle with the actual v0.3.0 identity/issuer policy; requires equal build identities and target SHA; rejects any build/PyInstaller step, moved publisher, self-approved deployment, collision, substitution, or candidate verdict; and fully validates this record before public transition. Failure removes only the just-created tag/draft before publication.
